summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Music Hub iteration 4 with 15 French artists and inline audio/video players. All backend APIs working perfectly (100% success rate). Frontend inline audio players (Deezer) and inline YouTube players functioning correctly. Only minor issue: 2 French artists need accent-insensitive search (Maître Gims, Édith Piaf work with lowercase names).

minor_issues.issue: Search normalization doesn't handle accents - 'Maître Gims' and 'Édith Piaf' need to be searched as 'maitre gims' and 'edith piaf'
minor_issues.impact: Users need to search without accents for these 2 artists

passed_tests: All 39 backend API endpoints (100% success rate)
Aya Nakamura returns exactly 4 anecdotes and 6 songs including 'Djadja'
Jul returns data about Marseille rapper with Marseille references
PNL returns data about the duo (Ademo & N.O.S references)
Nekfeu, Damso, Booba, Ninho, MC Solaar, IAM, Tiakola, Niska, Francis Cabrel all found
Orelsan returns data including 'Basique' song
Maître Gims found as 'maitre gims' with 4 anecdotes and 5 songs
Édith Piaf found as 'edith piaf' with 'La Vie en rose' data
13/15 French artists work with exact names, 2/15 work with normalized names
Deezer track preview plays inline with green play buttons on all artist pages
YouTube player opens inline (not new tab) when clicking 'Écouter' buttons
YouTube inline player has working close button
Audio players show 'playing' class indicating only one audio plays at a time
Artist pages show anecdotes and ghostwriting sections correctly
YouTube IDs present in songs: Ed Sheeran (4/9), Sia (7/9), Aya Nakamura (5/6), Orelsan (4/6)
Billboard Hot 100 current week and year-end charts working
Blind Test game with YouTube player and 4 answer choices working
Home page search autocomplete with artist navigation working
French language UI throughout the application
Navigation between all pages using header links working
Responsive design and proper styling maintained
action_item_for_main_agent: Optional: Improve artist search normalization to handle accents automatically (convert 'Maître Gims' to 'maitre gims' and 'Édith Piaf' to 'edith piaf' in search function). This is a minor UX improvement, not a critical bug.
